---
description: "Task list for Feature 009: Demo-Guide Hardening (LLM-Native Action Pipeline + Integrator-Ready Output)"
---

# Tasks: Demo-Guide Hardening (LLM-Native Action Pipeline + Integrator-Ready Output)

**Input**: Design documents from `specs/009-demo-guide-hardening/`
**Prerequisites**: [plan.md](plan.md), [spec.md](spec.md), [research.md](research.md), [data-model.md](data-model.md), [contracts/](contracts/), [quickstart.md](quickstart.md)

**Tests**: Spec FR-035 mandates a CI regression harness against `demo/shop`. Per-unit tests are NOT generated by default — only the FR-035 harness and the build-time template validation (FR-036) are scoped here. The spec explicitly preserves Constitution stance ("Tests are OPTIONAL").

**Organization**: Tasks grouped by user story so each P1 story can land independently and the demo regression harness (FR-035) can validate any subset.

## Format: `[ID] [P?] [Story] Description`

- **[P]**: Different files, no dependency on incomplete tasks → safe to parallelize.
- **[Story]**: `[US1]`..`[US6]` — maps directly to spec.md user stories. Setup, Foundational, Polish phases carry no story label.
- File paths are relative to repo root (`C:\Desarrollo\Personal\ai-to-widget`).

## Path Conventions

This is an npm-workspaces monorepo. Touch points:

- Scripts/orchestrator: `packages/scripts/src/` (compiled to `packages/scripts/dist/`).
- Backend templates: `packages/backend/src/`.
- Widget: `packages/widget/src/`.
- Demo shop: `demo/shop/`.
- Repo-root compose (to be relocated): `docker-compose.yml` → `tools/dev/docker-compose.yml`.
- Specs: `specs/009-demo-guide-hardening/`.
- Slash command docs: `commands/atw.*.md`.

---

## Phase 1: Setup (Shared Infrastructure)

**Purpose**: Verify environment, ensure existing dependencies cover Feature 009 needs (no new packages per Constitution VII), and stage the new file locations.

- [X] T001 Verify `packages/scripts/package.json` already declares `@anthropic-ai/sdk`, `@apidevtools/swagger-parser`, `zod`, `gray-matter`, `handlebars` (per plan.md Technical Context); no new dependency installations needed.
- [X] T002 [P] Create empty stub files where new modules will land: `packages/scripts/src/lib/llm-retry.ts`, `packages/scripts/src/lib/dist-staleness.ts`. Each contains only an `export {};` placeholder so subsequent tasks can edit-not-create.
- [X] T003 [P] Create stub directory `tools/dev/` (empty) so the FR-033 relocation lands cleanly without git-add ambiguity.

---

## Phase 2: Foundational (Blocking Prerequisites)

**Purpose**: Build the schema/contract/runtime-helper layer that every user story consumes. None of US1–US6 can ship correctly until this phase completes.

**⚠️ CRITICAL**: No user story work begins until this phase is done.

- [X] T004 Extend `packages/scripts/src/lib/pricing.ts` from a single `OPUS_PRICING` constant to a `MODEL_PRICING: Record<ModelSnapshot, PricingEntry>` map covering `claude-opus-4-7`, `claude-sonnet-4-6`, `claude-haiku-4-5`. Export a `ModelSnapshot` union type. (FR-006, R6)
- [X] T005 [P] Implement `packages/scripts/src/lib/llm-retry.ts`: an exponential-backoff helper with attempts=3, initial 500 ms, multiplier 2×, jitter ±20% seeded from `(model_snapshot + input_hash)`. Retryable: network/timeout/408/429/500/502/503/504. Non-retryable: 400/401/403/404 and post-call zod-validation failures. Records attempts + delays in a result object the caller appends to provenance. (FR-008a, R4)
- [X] T006 [P] Populate `packages/scripts/src/lib/runtime-config.ts` (currently empty placeholder per plan.md): a `loadProjectConfig(): ProjectConfig` reader for the YAML frontmatter in `.atw/config/project.md`, defaulting `model_snapshot` to `claude-opus-4-7` when absent. Validates against the project-md zod schema (T008). (FR-006, R6)
- [X] T007 [P] Add `packages/scripts/src/lib/schemas/action-manifest.ts`: zod schema mirroring `specs/009-demo-guide-hardening/contracts/action-manifest.schema.json`. Use `zod-to-json-schema` to derive the JSON schema from the zod definition (avoid hand-keeping two copies in sync). Export both `ActionManifest` (TS type) and `actionManifestJsonSchema` (the JSON form for the Anthropic tool-call `input_schema`). (FR-008, R3)
- [X] T008 [P] Add `packages/scripts/src/lib/schemas/project-md.ts`: zod schema mirroring `contracts/project-md.schema.json`. Validates origin URLs (`new URL()` parse), `model_snapshot` membership in `MODEL_PRICING` keys, deployment-conditional required fields (FR-009, FR-010, R6). Export `ProjectConfig` type and the runtime validator.
- [X] T009 [P] Add `packages/scripts/src/lib/schemas/build-provenance.ts`: zod schema mirroring `contracts/build-provenance.schema.json`. Includes the `status` enum (`success`/`success_cached`/`warning`/`skipped`/`failed`/`not_run`), `model_snapshot` requirement for CLASSIFY/ENRICH phases, conditional `skipped_reason`/`failed_reason`. (E3, FR-028)
- [X] T010 Extend `packages/scripts/src/lib/manifest-io.ts` with `readManifest(path)` and `writeManifest(path, manifest)` helpers that round-trip YAML frontmatter (machine fields) + markdown body (prose) using `gray-matter`. Validate frontmatter through the T007 zod schema on write; reject invalid manifests with field-level error paths. (FR-007, FR-008)
- [X] T011 [P] Implement `packages/scripts/src/lib/dist-staleness.ts`: walk `packages/scripts/src/**/*.ts`, compare each file's mtime to `packages/scripts/dist/<same-relative-path>.js`. Returns `{ stale: boolean, offendingFiles: string[] }`. Honors `ATW_SKIP_DIST_CHECK=1` env var (escape hatch for maintainer dev). (FR-032, R8)
- [X] T012 Wire the dist-staleness check into the entry point of every `packages/scripts/bin/atw-*.js` shim: on stale, print the "Run `npm run build`" message naming offending sources and exit non-zero. (FR-032, R8)

**Checkpoint**: Foundation ready. The schema layer, retry helper, runtime config, manifest I/O, and dist-staleness gate are all in place. User stories can now land in parallel.

---

## Phase 3: User Story 1 — Confirmed write executes end-to-end (Priority: P1) 🎯 MVP

**Goal**: A POST/PUT/PATCH against the host API, driven by the widget, lands with a correctly-shaped body. The manifest's `input_schema.properties` is non-empty for every write, populated from the source OpenAPI's request body schema.

**Independent Test**: Run `/atw.api` against an OpenAPI document containing at least one `POST` with `requestBody.$ref`. Inspect the resulting `action-manifest.md`: every write entry has non-empty `input_schema.properties` with the source schema's required + types. Drive the widget to confirm one write; the request body contains all required fields.

### Implementation for User Story 1

- [X] T013 [P] [US1] Refactor `packages/scripts/src/parse-openapi.ts` to use `@apidevtools/swagger-parser`'s `bundle()` (R1): inline external `$ref`s, preserve internal `$ref`s. Drop **all** semantic pre-filtering (token allowlists, path-segment heuristics, verb-table inference); retain Stage-1 structural filters only for `OPTIONS`/`HEAD`, no-response operations, and operations failing OpenAPI validation. (FR-001, FR-003, FR-004)
- [X] T014 [US1] Rewrite `packages/scripts/src/classify-actions.ts`: single `messages.create()` per source OpenAPI document via `tool_use` mode (R2). Tool definition: `emit_manifest` with `input_schema = actionManifestJsonSchema` (T007). User message contains `<project_md>`, `<openapi>` (bundled), `<output_schema>` blocks. Use `temperature: 0` for determinism. Wrap the call in the T005 retry helper. Disable Anthropic SDK built-in retries (`maxRetries: 0`). (FR-001, FR-002, FR-005, FR-008a, R4)
- [X] T015 [US1] In `classify-actions.ts`, post-call: validate the LLM's tool-call arguments through the T007 zod schema. Cross-check every `operations[].citation.operation_id` exists in the bundled source document. On failure (zod or cross-check), emit a phase-fail provenance entry with the field-level error path; do NOT retry (per FR-008a non-retryable list). (FR-002, FR-008)
- [X] T016 [US1] Extend `packages/scripts/src/write-manifest.ts` to write the validated manifest via the T010 round-trip helper. Header frontmatter records `schema_version: "1.0"`, `model_snapshot`, `input_hashes` (sha256 of bundled openapi + project_md), counts. (FR-005, FR-007, E1)
- [X] T017 [P] [US1] Update `packages/backend/src/routes/chat.ts.hbs` to consume the new manifest YAML-frontmatter shape (`operations[].http.method`, `path_template`, `input_schema`, `requires_confirmation`). Remove any code path that reads the old bold-bracketed inline-field markdown style. (FR-002, FR-007)
- [X] T018 [P] [US1] Update `packages/scripts/src/render-backend.ts` to pass the new manifest entries (including PUT verb — closes the project memory's PUT-template gap) to the chat route template. (FR-007, project memory `project_put_method_template_gap.md`)
- [X] T019 [P] [US1] Update `commands/atw.api.md`: rewrite the "Steps" section to describe the single LLM call with full bundled document + `project.md`, removing references to chunked-by-entity calls and pre-filter passes. Note that `/atw.api` and `/atw.classify` (spec naming) are the same command (R11).

**Checkpoint**: A confirmed write reaches the host API with a correctly populated body. SC-002 verifiable: every write in the manifest has non-empty `input_schema.properties`.

---

## Phase 4: User Story 2 — ActionCard says what is about to happen (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: When the assistant proposes a write, the ActionCard renders a complete English sentence with the actual argument values, substituted from `summary_template`. No raw tool name, no Opus-vague-question fallback.

**Independent Test**: For every write in the manifest, render the ActionCard for a sample tool-call with realistic arguments. Title row contains at least one argument value (product name, quantity, etc.).

### Implementation for User Story 2

- [X] T020 [P] [US2] Update `packages/widget/src/api-client.ts` (or the manifest-fetch type at the widget side): include `summary_template: string` and `requires_confirmation: boolean` per operation in the catalog response type. (FR-022, E1)
- [X] T021 [US2] Update `packages/widget/src/action-card.tsx`: substitute `{{ placeholder }}` tokens in `summary_template` with values from the proposed tool-call arguments. When a placeholder cannot be resolved (key missing from arguments), fall back to a deterministic sentence built from the tool name + present arguments — NOT a vague Opus description. (FR-022)
- [X] T022 [US2] Add inline tests in `packages/widget/src/action-card.tsx` (vitest) for two cases: (a) all placeholders resolved → exact substituted string rendered; (b) one placeholder missing → deterministic fallback rendered, raw tool name absent.

**Checkpoint**: SC-003 verifiable: 100% of ActionCards contain ≥1 argument value; zero fall back to raw tool name.

---

## Phase 5: User Story 3 — Embed guide produces a working integration on first paste (Priority: P1)

**Goal**: After `/atw.embed`, the integrator pastes the snippet, follows the doc verbatim, and the widget loads + reaches the host API for one read and one confirmed write — without any manual edit beyond what the guide tells them.

**Independent Test**: Fresh integrator with no ATW context follows `embed-guide.md` against a minimal host project. Widget loads, fetches catalog, accepts chat, reaches host API for read + confirmed write. Zero hand-edits to emitted artifacts.

> **Note**: US3 depends on US4's T024 (origin capture) for the inlined values. Develop in parallel; integrate when both are done.

### Implementation for User Story 3

- [X] T023 [P] [US3] Refactor `packages/scripts/src/embed.ts`: emit a single coherent `embed-guide.md` (merge the legacy Feature-004 body with the Feature-008 prepended summary into one document with one structure). Remove dual-template emission. (FR-012)
- [X] T024 [US3] Update `packages/scripts/src/embed-templates/{plain-html,custom,next-app-router,next-pages-router}.hbs`: inline every value from `project.md` (no `<your storefront origin>` placeholders, no `localhost` without explicit "REPLACE THIS" annotations, no leaked `specs/…` paths). The embed `<script>` tag MUST include `data-api-base-url="{{host_api_origin}}"` and `data-backend-url="{{atw_backend_origin}}"`. (FR-013, FR-014)
- [X] T025 [P] [US3] Remove `data-allowed-tools` attribute from all four embed templates. Document in the template comment block that the widget fetches the catalog from the ATW backend at boot. (FR-015)
- [X] T026 [US3] In each embed template, add a "CORS configuration" section that names `host_page_origin` verbatim, lists the headers the widget sends (`Authorization`, `Content-Type`), lists the verbs in the action manifest, and renders a framework-specific snippet (Express/cors, Fastify/@fastify/cors, Next.js middleware) keyed on what `/atw.init` detected — OR renders a checklist if no framework was detected. (FR-016)
- [X] T027 [US3] In each embed template, add a "Run the backend" section: container startup command, env-var list (`ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, `DATABASE_URL`, `ALLOWED_ORIGINS`), pgvector wiring note, link to the emitted `.env.example`. (FR-017, E5)
- [X] T028 [P] [US3] Extend `packages/scripts/src/embed.ts` to emit `<host>/atw/.env.example` with `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=`, `DATABASE_URL=postgresql://atw:atw@localhost:5432/atw`, `ALLOWED_ORIGINS={{host_page_origin}}`. The `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` line stays empty for the integrator. Comments explain ALLOWED_ORIGINS comma-separation. (FR-017, E5)
- [X] T029 [P] [US3] In each embed template, add a "Static assets" section that names the target folder for the detected stack (`public/` for Vite/Next.js/etc.), and includes a warning about build-time-frozen frontend containers needing rebuild before changes take effect. (FR-018)
- [X] T030 [P] [US3] In each embed template, audit every shell snippet: any bash-only command (`cp`, `export`, `chmod`) gets a paired PowerShell variant in a tabbed/sectioned block (e.g., "**bash** / **PowerShell**"). Cross-platform commands stay single-form. (FR-019)
- [X] T031 [P] [US3] In each embed template, remove any reference to `action-executors.json` that lacks an explanation of what it is and where it goes. Remove empty placeholder CSS blocks (`:root {}` with no tokens) — populate with the project's tokens or remove the section. (FR-020)
- [X] T032 [P] [US3] Update `packages/widget/src/api-client.ts` and `packages/widget/src/launcher.ts` to fetch the allowed-tool list from `${atw_backend_origin}/tools` at boot rather than reading `data-allowed-tools`. If the backend lacks `/tools` (FR-015 follow-up case), emit a one-time console warning and fall back to the shipped catalog file (per spec edge case "Builder declines to install runtime-discovery"). (FR-015)
- [X] T033 [US3] Update `packages/backend/src/routes/health.ts.hbs` (or add `tools.ts.hbs` handler) so the rendered backend exposes a `GET /tools` returning the catalog JSON. The rendered output is consumed by the widget's runtime fetch in T032. (FR-015)
- [X] T034 [P] [US3] Add a CI grep step (will live in T060) that asserts the emitted `embed-guide.md` contains none of: `<your storefront origin>`, `[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]`, `specs/`, bare `localhost` without "REPLACE THIS". Encode the grep patterns in a small script `packages/scripts/src/lib/embed-guide-lint.ts` consumed by both build-time and CI. (SC-006)

**Checkpoint**: Integrator runs the full path against a fresh host project and reaches a confirmed write without hand-edits. SC-006 verifiable via the lint helper.

---

## Phase 6: User Story 4 — `/atw.init` captures every input downstream phases need (Priority: P2)

**Goal**: One pass through `/atw.init` produces a `project.md` containing every value any later phase needs. No re-prompting, no placeholder leakage in emitted artifacts.

**Independent Test**: Run `/atw.init` on a fresh project; inspect `project.md`. Every value referenced in the templates emitted by `/atw.embed` and `/atw.build` is present (or explicitly marked optional with a documented default).

### Implementation for User Story 4

- [X] T035 [US4] Update `packages/scripts/src/init-project.ts` prompt sequence (deployment=`customer-facing-widget` branch): ask separately for `atw_backend_origin`, `host_api_origin`, `host_page_origin`, and the optional `login_url`. Each question shows an example value matching the spec's demo flow. (FR-009)
- [X] T036 [US4] In `init-project.ts`, after each origin answer, validate via `new URL(value)` parse. On parse failure, re-prompt with a clear "that doesn't look like a URL — try `http://localhost:3200`" message. Detect missing `://` (the `http//localhost:3200` typo case in spec acceptance scenario 2). (FR-010)
- [X] T037 [US4] In `init-project.ts`, after each origin is captured, optionally HEAD-request it: warn (don't block) if the server doesn't respond. (FR-010 SHOULD)
- [X] T038 [US4] Before `init-project.ts` writes `project.md`, validate the assembled config object through the T008 zod schema. If validation fails, abort the write and emit a message naming the outstanding questions. (FR-011)
- [X] T039 [P] [US4] Add a new `model_snapshot` prompt (default `claude-opus-4-7`) to the `/atw.init` flow. Validate membership in `MODEL_PRICING` keys (T004). Persist to `project.md` frontmatter. (FR-006, R6)
- [X] T040 [P] [US4] Update `commands/atw.init.md` to document the four origins and their purposes; clarify the "host API origin vs ATW backend origin" distinction.

**Checkpoint**: SC-006-relevant: a fresh `/atw.init` produces a `project.md` whose frontmatter validates against the contract; no template emitted in later phases needs a value missing from it.

---

## Phase 7: User Story 5 — `/atw.build` reports honest status, never silent failure (Priority: P2)

**Goal**: When a phase skips, fails, or produces zero rows, the integrator sees it. `✅ Build complete` is a strict assertion; anything else uses a different summary line.

**Independent Test**: Force each phase to fail one at a time (missing markers in host compose, zero rows in IMPORT, zero LLM calls in ENRICH, stale `dist/`). Each forced failure surfaces with a non-success summary and an actionable hint; downstream phases refuse to run.

### Implementation for User Story 5

- [X] T041 [US5] Refactor `packages/scripts/src/orchestrator.ts` to maintain a per-phase `BuildProvenanceEntry` object that conforms to the T009 schema. Append-write each entry to `.atw/artifacts/build-provenance.json` after the phase finishes. (E3, FR-028)
- [X] T042 [US5] In `orchestrator.ts`, replace the unconditional `✅ Build complete` line with a status-aware summary: only print `✅` when every phase entry has `status: success` or `success_cached`. On any `warning`/`skipped`/`failed`/`not_run`, print `⚠ Build complete with N warnings:` followed by per-phase lines naming the issue + actionable hint. (FR-028)
- [X] T043 [US5] In `orchestrator.ts`, before invoking each phase, check the previous run's provenance entry for matching `(input_hashes, model_snapshot)`. If found and prior status was `success`, mark the phase `success_cached` and skip; otherwise run. Failed phases (transients exhausted or schema-validation failure) MUST NOT be cached. (FR-008b)
- [X] T044 [US5] In `orchestrator.ts`, on phase failure or skip, compute a dynamic `next_hint` field in the provenance entry that points at the actual next required command (e.g., on COMPOSE skip → `Run /atw.build again and answer 'y' at the COMPOSE prompt`). No static hint strings. (FR-031)
- [X] T045 [P] [US5] Update `packages/scripts/src/compose-activate.ts`: when scanning the host compose file finds neither marker line (R7), prompt the integrator with `[y/N]` (default "no"). On `y`: append the marker block idempotently (re-runs are no-ops). On `n` or default: print the exact diff that would have been applied, plus the manual-fix command, and return a `skipped` status with `skipped_reason` populated. ATW MUST NOT modify the host compose file without explicit confirmation. (FR-029, Q3, R7)
- [X] T046 [P] [US5] Add `packages/scripts/src/lib/cost-estimator.ts` (or extend the existing one): given `(bundledOpenapi, projectMd, modelSnapshot)`, call Anthropic's `messages.countTokens()` for the input, apply the `100 × in-scope operation count` heuristic for output, multiply by `MODEL_PRICING[snapshot]`, return USD estimate + breakdown. (FR-006a, R5)
- [X] T047 [US5] In `orchestrator.ts` (and in `classify-actions.ts` directly), call the cost estimator before each LLM call and print the line `[<phase>] OpenAPI: <N> operations | model: <snapshot> | est. cost: ~$<X.XX> (continuing in 2s, Ctrl+C to abort)`. Then `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 2000))` and proceed. No `[y/N]` prompt; no `--yes` flag introduced. (FR-006a, Q5)
- [X] T048 [P] [US5] Extend each `/atw.*` command shim's precondition check (in `packages/scripts/src/orchestrator.ts` or per-bin entry): refuse to run if the upstream artifact required by FR-031 is missing, naming the missing precondition AND the actual next required command (not a static string). (FR-031)
- [X] T049 [P] [US5] Update `commands/atw.build.md` to document the new status taxonomy (`success`/`warning`/`skipped`/`failed`/`success_cached`) and that the COMPOSE phase will prompt `[y/N]`.

**Checkpoint**: SC-004 verifiable: forced-failure tests trigger non-success summaries with actionable hints in 100% of cases.

---

## Phase 8: User Story 6 — Widget UX is consistent and unblocking (Priority: P3)

**Goal**: Single language-neutral waiting indicator. Citations gone. Panel doesn't block host-page interaction.

**Independent Test**: Open the widget on a host page with scrollable content. (a) Waiting state uses one text-free indicator. (b) No citation UI renders, no `Citation[]` data in payloads. (c) Host page receives scroll/click/hover normally with panel open.

### Implementation for User Story 6

- [X] T050 [P] [US6] Remove Spanish loading strings from `packages/widget/src/message-list.tsx` (and any state.ts paths that emit them): `"Obteniendo datos…"`, `"Datos obtenidos, interpretando…"`. Replace with the existing language-neutral 3-dot animation for both backend wait and tool execution. (FR-021)
- [X] T051 [US6] Update any widget tests pinning the Spanish literals to assert on the language-neutral indicator instead. Locate via grep for the Spanish strings under `packages/widget/`. (FR-021)
- [X] T052 [P] [US6] Delete the `turnCitations` WeakMap and all citation rendering code from `packages/widget/src/state.ts` and `packages/widget/src/message-list.tsx`. Delete citation-pill components and related CSS rules in `packages/widget/src/styles.css`. (FR-023, Q1, R9)
- [X] T053 [P] [US6] Delete the `Citation[]` field (and its zod schema) from `packages/widget/src/api-client.ts` types. (FR-023, FR-024, Q1)
- [X] T054 [US6] Delete the `Citation[]` field from `packages/backend/src/routes/chat.ts.hbs` response schema. Delete citation assembly code in the same file and the `firstTitle()` helper. (FR-024, Q1)
- [X] T055 [P] [US6] Delete citation-side filtering from `packages/backend/src/retrieval.ts.hbs`: the cosine-similarity threshold for the *citation set* and the "only Opus-cited" filter pass. Retrieval still feeds context into the prompt for grounding (Constitution V satisfied prompt-side). (FR-025, Q1, R9)
- [X] T056 [P] [US6] Remove the `suggestions` field entirely from `packages/backend/src/routes/chat.ts.hbs`'s `NormalChatResponseSchema` (zod) and from any TS type that mirrors it on the widget side. Compile-time enforcement prevents future re-introduction without a schema change. (FR-026)
- [X] T057 [P] [US6] Update `packages/widget/src/panel.tsx` to remove the modal backdrop element, the global focus trap (any `useFocusTrap`/`tabIndex` global handlers), and any `body { overflow: hidden }` style applied while the panel is open. The panel renders inline-positioned (fixed/absolute) without intercepting host-page events outside its bounds. (FR-027)
- [X] T058 [US6] Add a vitest spec in `packages/widget/src/panel.test.tsx` (new file) asserting: with the panel mounted in a test DOM, a click on `document.body` outside the panel's bounding rect dispatches normally to the body's listener. (FR-027 verification)

**Checkpoint**: All P3 friction items (D1a, D2a–D2f from the friction memory) closed. Test environment confirms host-page click-through.

---

## Phase 9: Polish & Cross-Cutting Concerns

**Purpose**: Improvements spanning multiple stories — pg_dump 17/18 compatibility, repo-root compose relocation, demo/shop CI, build-time template validation, narrative cleanup.

- [X] T059 [P] Update `packages/scripts/src/import-dump.ts` to handle pg_dump 17/18 constructs by default: strip `SET transaction_timeout = …;` lines, strip `\restrict`/`\unrestrict` psql metacommands, and strip (or rewrite-to-skip) `ALTER TABLE … OWNER TO …;` clauses when the target user doesn't exist. Add a fixture under `packages/scripts/test/fixtures/pg_dump_17.sql` exhibiting these constructs and assert the importer succeeds. (FR-030, R12)
- [X] T060 Add a build-time template validation step. New file: `packages/scripts/src/lib/validate-templates.ts`. For each `.hbs` under `packages/backend/src/` and `packages/scripts/src/embed-templates/`, render against a canonical fixture context, then run `tsc --noEmit` (or `esbuild --bundle --no-emit`) against the rendered TS output. Fail on type errors. Wire into `npm run build` (workspaces) so renders happen before publishing `dist/`. (FR-036, R13)
- [X] T061 Move the repo-root `docker-compose.yml` to `tools/dev/docker-compose.yml`. Remove the stale mount path `./demo/atw-shop-host/atw.sql` (retired per FR-033). Use `git mv` to preserve history. (FR-033, R10)
- [X] T062 [P] Create `tools/dev/README.md` (≤30 lines) explaining: this directory is for ATW maintainers only; integrators following the embed guide do not touch anything here; the compose here spins up Postgres + a dev backend for local ATW development; pointer to the embed guide. (FR-033, E6)
- [X] T063 [P] Update `README.md`, `TESTING-GUIDE.md`, `Makefile`, and any inline references that point to the old repo-root `docker-compose.yml` path. Search via grep for `docker-compose.yml` and audit each hit. (FR-033)
- [ ] T064 [P] Update `demo/shop/docker-compose.yml`: set `ALLOWED_ORIGINS` to match the frontend container's exposed origin (e.g., `http://localhost:8080`). The demo never regresses to "CORS error" on confirmed writes. (FR-034) — DEFERRED: `demo/shop/docker-compose.yml` does not exist on disk yet (demo/shop currently ships only as built dist artefacts). Will be addressed when `demo/shop` is scaffolded with source.
- [ ] T065 Add `.github/workflows/atw-regression.yml`: triggered on PRs touching `packages/scripts/**`, `demo/shop/**`, or `specs/009-demo-guide-hardening/**`. Steps: setup Node 20, `npm ci`, `npm run build`, start `demo/shop` Postgres + backend in Docker, run a scripted equivalent of Steps 1–7 in `quickstart.md` against the demo OpenAPI fixture (Anthropic key from repo secret). Assertions: SC-002 (every write has non-empty `input_schema.properties`), SC-003 (every ActionCard contains ≥1 argument value), SC-004 (forced-failure scenarios trigger non-success summaries), SC-006 (embed-guide grep finds no placeholders). (FR-035, R14) — DEFERRED: depends on T064 (demo/shop scaffolding).
- [ ] T066 [P] Manually walk through `quickstart.md` Steps 1–8 against `demo/shop` from a fresh terminal. Time the full run; confirm ≤30 minutes, zero hand-edits, zero outside-the-guide debugging. (SC-001 manual verification before merge) — pending manual run by user.
- [X] T067 [P] Update `CLAUDE.md` to keep the SPECKIT-bracketed reference current (already done by `/speckit-plan`; verify); spot-check that no stale paths leak.
- [X] T068 [P] Run `npm run lint` and fix any lint warnings introduced by the refactor; ensure `npm run build` succeeds end-to-end.

---

## Dependencies & Execution Order

### Phase Dependencies

- **Setup (Phase 1)** → no dependencies; can start immediately.
- **Foundational (Phase 2)** → depends on Setup; **blocks all user stories**.
- **US1 (Phase 3, P1)** → depends on Foundational; emits the manifest US2/US5 consume.
- **US2 (Phase 4, P1)** → depends on Foundational; consumes the new manifest schema (T020 reads what T015 wrote). May run after T015 is done even if rest of US1 still in flight.
- **US3 (Phase 5, P1)** → depends on Foundational; the embed template inlining (T024) consumes `project.md` fields whose population is US4's T035. Develop in parallel; **integration test requires both**.
- **US4 (Phase 6, P2)** → depends on Foundational; sibling of US3.
- **US5 (Phase 7, P2)** → depends on Foundational; T043 (input-hash cache) consumes hashes recorded by US1's T016.
- **US6 (Phase 8, P3)** → depends on Foundational only. Independent of US1–US5 (citation removal touches isolated files).
- **Polish (Phase 9)** → mostly depends on user-story phases being complete; T059 (pg_dump) and T064 (demo compose) and T061 (compose relocation) can land earlier independently.

### Within Each User Story

- Schemas (Foundational T007–T009) before consumers.
- Manifest writer (T016) before consumers (T017, T018, T020).
- Init prompts (T035) before integration test of US3.
- Provenance writer (T041) before status-aware summary (T042) before cache-skip logic (T043).

### Parallel Opportunities

- All `[P]` tasks in Phase 1 in parallel.
- Phase 2: T005, T006, T007, T008, T009, T011 all `[P]` — six developers can each take one.
- Phase 3 internal: T013, T017, T018, T019 are `[P]` once T014/T015 are merged.
- Phase 5 internal: T024–T031 are mostly `[P]` (separate template files / separate concerns).
- Phase 8: T050, T052, T053, T055, T056, T057 are `[P]` (different files).
- Phase 9: T059, T062, T063, T064, T066, T067, T068 are `[P]`.
- Cross-phase: US6 (Phase 8) can run completely in parallel with US1/US3/US4/US5 — no shared files.

---

## Parallel Example: User Story 1 implementation kickoff

```bash
# After Foundational checkpoint (T012 done):

# Developer A — pipeline core
Task: T013 Refactor parse-openapi.ts to bundle()+structural-only filters
Task: T014 Rewrite classify-actions.ts to single tool-use call

# Developer B — manifest plumbing (after T014/T015 merge)
Task: T016 Extend write-manifest.ts to write the validated manifest

# Developer C — backend consumer (after T016 merge)
Task: T017 Update chat.ts.hbs to consume new YAML-frontmatter shape
Task: T018 Update render-backend.ts to pass new manifest entries
```

## Parallel Example: User Story 6 (independent of US1–US5)

```bash
# As soon as Foundational checkpoint completes, US6 can run in parallel:

Task: T050 Remove Spanish loading strings from message-list.tsx
Task: T052 Delete turnCitations WeakMap from state.ts
Task: T053 Delete Citation[] field from api-client.ts types
Task: T056 Remove suggestions field from NormalChatResponseSchema
Task: T057 Remove modal backdrop / focus trap from panel.tsx
```

---

## Implementation Strategy

### MVP First (User Story 1 only)

1. Complete Phase 1: Setup.
2. Complete Phase 2: Foundational (CRITICAL — blocks all stories).
3. Complete Phase 3: US1 — confirmed write executes end-to-end.
4. **STOP and VALIDATE**: run the manifest-emission flow against the demo OpenAPI fixture; confirm SC-002 (every write has non-empty `input_schema.properties`).
5. Demo or freeze MVP at this point.

### Incremental Delivery

1. Setup + Foundational → foundation ready.
2. US1 → MVP demo (confirmed write works).
3. US2 → ActionCard summary visible to shoppers.
4. US3 + US4 (developed in parallel) → embed guide produces working integration on first paste.
5. US5 → build status honest.
6. US6 → widget UX clean (citations gone, click-through, neutral indicator).
7. Polish → CI regression + repo-root compose relocation + pg_dump 17/18 + manual quickstart walk.

### Parallel Team Strategy

After Foundational checkpoint:
- Developer A: US1 (the rector core).
- Developer B: US3 + US4 (embed + init).
- Developer C: US5 (build orchestration).
- Developer D: US6 (widget UX cleanup — independent).
- Developer E (after US1 emits manifest): US2.

US1 unblocks US2 and US5 (cache-skip logic). US3 + US4 can integrate when both reach their checkpoints.

---

## Notes

- `[P]` = different files, no dependency on incomplete tasks.
- `[Story]` label maps each task to a user story for traceability.
- Constitution Check (in plan.md) passes; no entries in Complexity Tracking. Tasks introduce no new datastore, no new framework, no new language runtime.
- All five 2026-04-25 clarifications are reflected: Q1 (citations removed) → US6; Q2 (retry policy) → T005; Q3 (`[y/N]` COMPOSE) → T045; Q4 (refuse on stale dist) → T011/T012; Q5 (informational countdown) → T046/T047.
- Project memory's PUT-template gap closes naturally at T018 (the new YAML-frontmatter manifest carries the verb explicitly; templates render whatever method the manifest declares).
- Verify each phase's checkpoint before moving on; commit after each task or logical group.
- Avoid: vague tasks, same-file conflicts across `[P]` tasks, cross-story dependencies that break independent testing.
